			<td id=".symb.7" style="width: 1%; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin-left: 0pt;">$</td>
			<td id=".amt.7" style="width: 6%; text-align: right; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin-left: 0pt;">35.69</td>
			<td id=".trail.7" nowrap="true" style="width: 1%; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin-left: 0pt;">&nbsp;</td>
			<td id=".lead.8" style="width: 1%; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;">&nbsp;</td>
			<td id=".symb.8" style="width: 1%; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; border-bottom: 3px double rgb(0, 0, 0);">&nbsp;</td>
			<td id=".amt.8" style="width: 6%; text-align: right; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin-left: 0pt; border-bottom: 3px double rgb(0, 0, 0);">4,633</td>
			<td id=".trail.8" nowrap="true" style="width: 1%; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; padding-bottom: 3px; margin-left: 0pt;">&nbsp;</td>
			<td id=".lead.9" style="width: 1%; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;">&nbsp;</td>
			<td id=".symb.9" style="width: 1%; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin-left: 0pt;">$</td>
			<td id=".amt.9" style="width: 6%; text-align: right; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin-left: 0pt;">50.94</td>
		</tr>

</table>
</div>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<hr style="text-align: left; height: 1px; color: #000000; background-color: #000000; width: 10%; border: none; margin: 3pt auto 3pt 0">
<table border="0" cellpadding="0" cellspacing="0" style="width:100%;text-indent:0;font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;">

		<tr>
			<td style="vertical-align:top;width:3.3%;">
			<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">(1)</p>
			</td>
			<td style="vertical-align:top;width:96.7%;">
			<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">Amount reflects the number of PSUs and MPSUs that&nbsp;may&nbsp;ultimately be earned based on management&rsquo;s probability assessment of the achievement of performance conditions at each reporting period. In addition, MPSUs are subject to the achievement of market conditions.</p>
			</td>
		</tr>

</table>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt 7.5pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin-bottom:0pt;margin-left:0pt;margin-right:7.5pt;margin-top:0pt;text-align:left;">The intrinsic&nbsp;value related to vested RSUs was $90.0 million, $74.0 million and $62.9 million for the years ended December 31, 2018, 2017 and 2016, respectively. As of December 31, 2018, the total intrinsic value of all outstanding&nbsp;RSUs was $494.0 million, based on the closing stock price of $116.25. As of December 31, 2018, unamortized compensation expense related to&nbsp;all outstanding RSUs was $124.5 million with a weighted-average remaining recognition period of approximately 3.5&nbsp;years.&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt 7.5pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">Cash proceeds from vested PSUs with a purchase price totaled&nbsp;$10.6&nbsp;million for the&nbsp;year ended&nbsp;December 31, 2018.&nbsp;There were&nbsp;no&nbsp;proceeds for the&nbsp;years ended December 31, 2017 and 2016.</p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;"><i><b>&nbsp;</b></i></p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;"><i><b>Time-Based RSUs</b></i></p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">For the years ended December 31, 2018, 2017 and 2016, the Compensation Committee granted 133,000, 81,000 and 133,000 RSUs, respectively, with service conditions&nbsp;to non-executive employees and non-employee directors. The RSUs vest over&nbsp;four&nbsp;years for employees and&nbsp;one&nbsp;year for directors, subject to continued service&nbsp;with the Company.</p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt 7.5pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin-bottom:0pt;margin-left:0pt;margin-right:7.5pt;margin-top:0pt;text-align:left;"><i><b>PSUs and MPSUs</b></i></p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt 7.5pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;"><u>2019 PSUs:</u></p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">In&nbsp;October 2018,&nbsp;the Compensation Committee&nbsp;granted 53,000&nbsp;PSUs to certain non-executive employees, which represent a target number of&nbsp;shares to be earned based on the Company&rsquo;s&nbsp;2020&nbsp;revenue goals for certain regions or product line divisions, or based on the Company&rsquo;s average&nbsp;two-year (2019&nbsp;and&nbsp;2020) revenue growth rate compared against the analog industry&rsquo;s average&nbsp;two-year revenue growth rate as published by the Semiconductor Industry Association (&ldquo;2019&nbsp;Non-Executive PSUs&rdquo;). The maximum number of shares that an employee can earn is either&nbsp;200%&nbsp;or&nbsp;300%&nbsp;of the target number of the&nbsp;2019&nbsp;Non-Executive PSUs, depending on the job classification of the employee.&nbsp;50%&nbsp;of the&nbsp;2019&nbsp;Non-Executive PSUs will vest in the&nbsp;first&nbsp;quarter of&nbsp;2021&nbsp;if the pre-determined performance goals are met during the performance period. The remaining&nbsp;2019&nbsp;Non-Executive PSUs&nbsp;will vest over the following&nbsp;two&nbsp;years on an annual or quarterly basis. Assuming the achievement of the highest level of performance goals, the total stock-based compensation cost for the&nbsp;2019&nbsp;Non-Executive PSUs is&nbsp;$10.9&nbsp;million.</p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt 7.5pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin-bottom:0pt;margin-left:0pt;margin-right:7.5pt;margin-top:0pt;text-align:left;">The&nbsp;2019&nbsp;Non-Executive PSUs contain a purchase price feature, which requires the employees to pay the Company&nbsp;$30&nbsp;per share upon vesting of the shares.&nbsp;Shares that do&nbsp;not&nbsp;vest will&nbsp;not&nbsp;be subject to the purchase price payment. The Company determined the grant date fair value of the&nbsp;2019&nbsp;Non-Executive PSUs using the Black-Scholes model with the following assumptions: stock price of&nbsp;$108.43,&nbsp;expected term of&nbsp;2.9&nbsp;years, expected volatility of&nbsp;28.7%&nbsp;and risk-free interest rate of&nbsp;2.9%.&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt 7.5pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;"><u>2018 PSUs:</u></p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">In&nbsp;February 2018,&nbsp;the Compensation Committee&nbsp;granted&nbsp;188,000&nbsp;PSUs to the executive officers, which represent a target number of shares to be earned based on the Company&rsquo;s average&nbsp;two-year (2018&nbsp;and&nbsp;2019) revenue growth rate compared against the analog industry&rsquo;s average&nbsp;two-year revenue growth rate as published by the Semiconductor Industry Association (&ldquo;2018&nbsp;Executive PSUs&rdquo;). The maximum number of shares that an executive officer can earn is&nbsp;300%&nbsp;of the target number of the&nbsp;2018&nbsp;Executive PSUs.&nbsp;50%&nbsp;of the&nbsp;2018&nbsp;Executive PSUs will vest in the&nbsp;first&nbsp;quarter of&nbsp;2020&nbsp;if the pre-determined performance goals are met during the&nbsp;performance period. The remaining&nbsp;2018&nbsp;Executive PSUs&nbsp;will vest over the following&nbsp;two&nbsp;years on a quarterly basis. Assuming the achievement of the highest level of performance goals, the total stock-based compensation cost for the&nbsp;2018&nbsp;Executive PSUs is&nbsp;$46.1&nbsp;million.</p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<div id="PGBK" style="width: 100%; margin-left: 0pt; margin-right: 0pt">
<div id="PGNUM" style="text-align: center; width: 100%; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;">62</div>

<hr style="PAGE-BREAK-AFTER: always; border: none; width: 100%; height: 2px; color: #000000; background-color: #000000">
<div id="PGHDR" style="text-align: left; width: 100%"><a href="#toc">Table of Contents</a></div>
</div>

<p style="font-family: 'Times New Roman', Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: left">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">In&nbsp;February 2018,&nbsp;the Compensation Committee&nbsp;granted&nbsp;44,000&nbsp;PSUs to certain non-executive employees, which represent a target number of&nbsp;shares to be earned based on the Company&rsquo;s&nbsp;2019&nbsp;revenue goals for certain regions or product line divisions, or based on the Company&rsquo;s average&nbsp;two-year (2018&nbsp;and&nbsp;2019) revenue growth rate compared against the analog industry&rsquo;s average&nbsp;two-year revenue growth rate as published by the Semiconductor Industry Association (&ldquo;2018&nbsp;Non-Executive PSUs&rdquo;). The maximum number of shares that an employee can earn is either&nbsp;200%&nbsp;or&nbsp;300%&nbsp;of the target number of the&nbsp;2018&nbsp;Non-Executive PSUs, depending on the job classification of the employee.&nbsp;50%&nbsp;of the&nbsp;2018&nbsp;Non-Executive PSUs will vest in the&nbsp;first&nbsp;quarter of&nbsp;2020&nbsp;if the pre-determined performance goals are met during the performance period. The remaining&nbsp;2018&nbsp;Non-Executive PSUs&nbsp;will vest over the following&nbsp;two&nbsp;years on an annual or quarterly basis. Assuming the achievement of the highest level of performance goals, the total stock-based compensation cost for the&nbsp;2018&nbsp;Non-Executive PSUs, excluding cancelled shares for the terminated employees, is&nbsp;$8.8&nbsp;million.</p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">The&nbsp;2018&nbsp;Executive PSUs and the&nbsp;2018&nbsp;Non-Executive PSUs contain a purchase price feature, which requires the employees to pay the Company&nbsp;$30&nbsp;per share upon vesting of the shares.&nbsp;Shares that do&nbsp;not&nbsp;vest will&nbsp;not&nbsp;be subject to the purchase price payment. The Company determined the grant date fair value of the&nbsp;2018&nbsp;Executive PSUs and the&nbsp;2018&nbsp;Non-Executive PSUs using the Black-Scholes model with the following assumptions: stock price of&nbsp;$110.00,&nbsp;expected term of&nbsp;2.6&nbsp;years, expected volatility of&nbsp;27.5%&nbsp;and risk-free interest rate of&nbsp;2.3%.&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin-bottom:0pt;margin-left:0pt;margin-right:7.5pt;margin-top:0pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin-bottom:0pt;margin-left:0pt;margin-right:7.5pt;margin-top:0pt;text-align:left;"><u>2017 PSUs:</u></p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt 7.5pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin-bottom:0pt;margin-left:0pt;margin-right:7.5pt;margin-top:0pt;text-align:left;">In February 2017, the Compensation Committee&nbsp;granted 200,000&nbsp;PSUs to the executive officers, which represented a target number of shares that would be earned based on the Company&rsquo;s average two-year (2017 and 2018) revenue growth rate compared against the analog industry&rsquo;s average two-year revenue growth rate as published by the Semiconductor Industry Association (&ldquo;2017 Executive PSUs&rdquo;). The maximum number of shares that an executive officer could earn was 300% of the target number of the 2017 Executive PSUs.&nbsp;In&nbsp;February 2019,&nbsp;the Compensation Committee&nbsp;approved the revenue achievement for the&nbsp;2017&nbsp;Executive PSUs and a total of&nbsp;521,000&nbsp;shares were earned by the executive officers. 50% of the 2017 Executive PSUs vested in the first quarter of 2019. The remaining&nbsp;2017 Executive PSUs&nbsp;vest over the following two years on a quarterly basis. Based on the actual achievement of the performance goals, the total stock-based compensation cost for the&nbsp;2017&nbsp;Executive PSUs is&nbsp;$31.5&nbsp;million.</p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin-bottom:0pt;margin-left:0pt;margin-right:7.5pt;margin-top:0pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin-bottom:0pt;margin-left:0pt;margin-right:7.5pt;margin-top:0pt;text-align:left;">In February 2017, the Compensation Committee&nbsp;granted 48,000&nbsp;PSUs to certain non-executive employees, which represented a target number of&nbsp;shares that would be earned based on the Company&rsquo;s 2018 revenue goals for certain regions or product line divisions, or based on the Company&rsquo;s average two-year (2017 and 2018) revenue growth rate compared against the analog industry&rsquo;s average two-year revenue growth rate as published by the Semiconductor Industry Association (&ldquo;2017 Non-Executive PSUs&rdquo;). The maximum number of shares that an employee could earn was either 200% or 300% of the target number of the 2017 Non-Executive PSUs, depending on the job classification of the employee. In&nbsp;February 2019,&nbsp;the Compensation Committee approved the revenue achievement for the&nbsp;2017&nbsp;Non-Executive PSUs and a total of&nbsp;101,000&nbsp;shares were earned by the employees. 50% of the 2017 Non-Executive PSUs vested in the first quarter of 2019. The remaining&nbsp;2017 Non-Executive PSUs&nbsp;vest over the following two years on an annual or quarterly basis. Based on the actual achievement of the performance goals, the total stock-based compensation cost for the&nbsp;2017&nbsp;Non-Executive PSUs, excluding cancelled shares for the terminated employees, is&nbsp;$6.1&nbsp;million.</p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt 7.5pt;text-align:left;"><i><b>&nbsp;</b></i></p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin-bottom:0pt;margin-left:0pt;margin-right:7.5pt;margin-top:0pt;text-align:left;">The 2017 Executive PSUs and the 2017 Non-Executive PSUs contain a purchase price feature, which requires the employees to pay the Company $30 per share upon vesting of the shares.&nbsp;Shares that do not vest will not be subject to the purchase price payment. The Company determined the grant date fair value of the 2017 Executive PSUs and the 2017 Non-Executive PSUs using the Black-Scholes model with the following assumptions: stock price of $89.37, expected term of 2.6 years, expected volatility of 28.6% and risk-free interest rate of 1.3%.&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt 7.5pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin-bottom:0pt;margin-left:0pt;margin-right:7.5pt;margin-top:0pt;text-align:left;"><u>2016 PSUs:</u></p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin-bottom:0pt;margin-left:0pt;margin-right:7.5pt;margin-top:0pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin-bottom:0pt;margin-left:0pt;margin-right:7.5pt;margin-top:0pt;text-align:left;">In February 2016, the Compensation Committee granted 285,000&nbsp;PSUs to the executive officers, and in July 2016, the Compensation Committee&nbsp;granted 12,000 PSUs to the Company&rsquo;s new Chief Financial Officer, which represented a target number of shares that would be earned based on the Company&rsquo;s average two-year (2016 and 2017) revenue growth rate compared against the analog industry&rsquo;s average two-year revenue growth rate as published by the Semiconductor Industry Association (&ldquo;2016 Executive PSUs&rdquo;). The maximum number of shares that an executive officer could earn was 300% of the target number of the 2016 Executive PSUs.&nbsp;In February 2018, the Compensation Committee&nbsp;approved the revenue achievement for the 2016 Executive PSUs and a total of 651,000 shares were earned by the executive officers. 50% of the 2016 Executive PSUs vested in the first quarter of 2018. The remaining&nbsp;2016 Executive PSUs&nbsp;vest over the following two years on a quarterly basis. Based on the actual achievement of the performance goals, the total stock-based compensation cost for the 2016 Executive PSUs, excluding cancelled shares for the Company&rsquo;s former Chief Financial Officer, is $26.1 million.</p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin-bottom:0pt;margin-left:0pt;margin-right:7.5pt;margin-top:0pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin-bottom:0pt;margin-left:0pt;margin-right:7.5pt;margin-top:0pt;text-align:left;">In February 2016, the Compensation Committee&nbsp;granted 64,000&nbsp;PSUs to certain non-executive employees, which represented a target number of&nbsp;shares that would be earned based on the Company&rsquo;s 2017 revenue goals for certain regions or product line divisions, or based on the Company&rsquo;s average two-year (2016 and 2017) revenue growth rate compared against the analog industry&rsquo;s average two-year revenue growth rate as published by the Semiconductor Industry Association (&ldquo;2016 Non-Executive PSUs&rdquo;). The maximum number of shares that an employee could earn was either 200% or 300% of the target number of the 2016 Non-Executive PSUs, depending on the job classification of the employee. In February 2018, the Compensation Committee approved the revenue achievement for the 2016 Non-Executive PSUs and a total of 128,000 shares were earned by the employees. 50% of the 2016 Non-Executive PSUs vested in the first quarter of 2018. The remaining&nbsp;2016 Non-Executive PSUs&nbsp;vest over the following two years on an annual or quarterly basis. Based on the actual achievement of the performance goals, the total stock-based compensation cost for the 2016 Non-Executive PSUs, excluding cancelled shares for the terminated employees, is $5.1&nbsp;million.</p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt 7.5pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<div id="PGBK" style="width: 100%; margin-left: 0pt; margin-right: 0pt">
<div id="PGNUM" style="text-align: center; width: 100%; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;">63</div>

<hr style="PAGE-BREAK-AFTER: always; border: none; width: 100%; height: 2px; color: #000000; background-color: #000000">
<div id="PGHDR" style="text-align: left; width: 100%"><a href="#toc">Table of Contents</a></div>
</div>

<p style="font-family: 'Times New Roman', Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: left">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin-bottom:0pt;margin-left:0pt;margin-right:7.5pt;margin-top:0pt;text-align:left;">The 2016 Executive PSUs and the 2016 Non-Executive PSUs contain a purchase price feature, which requires the employees to pay the Company $20 per share upon vesting of the shares.&nbsp;Shares that do not vest will not be subject to the purchase price payment. The Company determined the grant date fair value of the 2016 Executive PSUs and the 2016 Non-Executive PSUs granted in February 2016 using the Black-Scholes model with the following assumptions: stock price of $58.98, expected term of 2.6 years, expected volatility of 31.1% and risk-free interest rate of 0.9%.&nbsp;For the&nbsp;2016 Executive PSUs&nbsp;granted in July 2016, the Company used the following assumptions: stock price of $70.98, expected term of 2.3 years, expected volatility of 29.6% and risk-free interest rate of 0.7%.</p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt 7.5pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;"><u>2015</u><u>&nbsp;PSUs:</u></p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">In&nbsp;February 2015,&nbsp;the Compensation Committee&nbsp;granted&nbsp;172,000&nbsp;PSUs to the executive officers, which represented a target number of shares that would be earned based on the Company&rsquo;s average&nbsp;two-year (2015&nbsp;and&nbsp;2016) revenue growth rate compared against the analog industry&rsquo;s average&nbsp;two-year revenue growth rate as published by the Semiconductor Industry Association (&ldquo;2015&nbsp;Executive PSUs&rdquo;). The maximum number of shares that an executive officer could earn was&nbsp;300%&nbsp;of the target number of the&nbsp;2015&nbsp;Executive PSUs.&nbsp;In&nbsp;February 2017,&nbsp;the Compensation Committee&nbsp;approved the revenue achievement for the&nbsp;2015&nbsp;Executive PSUs and a total of&nbsp;432,000&nbsp;shares were earned by the executive officers.&nbsp;50%&nbsp;of the&nbsp;2015&nbsp;Executive PSUs vested in the&nbsp;first&nbsp;quarter of&nbsp;2017.&nbsp;The remaining&nbsp;2015&nbsp;Executive PSUs vest over the following&nbsp;two&nbsp;years on a quarterly basis. Based on the actual achievement of the performance goals, the total stock-based compensation cost for the&nbsp;2015&nbsp;Executive PSUs, excluding cancelled shares for the Company&rsquo;s former Chief Financial Officer, is&nbsp;$21.0&nbsp;million.</p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">In&nbsp;February 2015,&nbsp;the Compensation Committee&nbsp;granted&nbsp;58,000&nbsp;PSUs to certain non-executive employees, which represented a target number of shares that would be earned based on the Company&rsquo;s&nbsp;2016&nbsp;revenue goals for certain regions or product line divisions, or based on the Company&rsquo;s average&nbsp;two-year (2015&nbsp;and&nbsp;2016) revenue growth rate compared against the analog industry&rsquo;s average&nbsp;two-year revenue growth rate as published by the Semiconductor Industry Association (&ldquo;2015&nbsp;Non-Executive PSUs&rdquo;). The maximum number of shares that an employee could earn was either&nbsp;200%&nbsp;or&nbsp;300%&nbsp;of the target number of the&nbsp;2015&nbsp;Non-Executive PSUs, depending on the job classification of the employee.&nbsp;In&nbsp;February 2017,&nbsp;the Compensation Committee&nbsp;approved the revenue achievement for the&nbsp;2015&nbsp;Non-Executive PSUs and a total of&nbsp;118,000&nbsp;shares were earned by the&nbsp;employees.&nbsp;50%&nbsp;of the&nbsp;2015&nbsp;Non-Executive PSUs vested in the&nbsp;first&nbsp;quarter of&nbsp;2017.&nbsp;The remaining&nbsp;2015&nbsp;Non-Executive PSUs vest over the following&nbsp;two&nbsp;years on an annual or quarterly basis. Based on the actual achievement of the performance goals, the total stock-based compensation cost for the&nbsp;2015&nbsp;Non-Executive PSUs, excluding cancelled shares for the terminated employees, is&nbsp;$5.7&nbsp;million.</p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin-bottom:0pt;margin-left:0pt;margin-right:7.5pt;margin-top:0pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin-bottom:0pt;margin-left:0pt;margin-right:7.5pt;margin-top:0pt;text-align:left;"><u>2015 MPSUs:</u></p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt 7.5pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">In December 2015,&nbsp;the Compensation Committee&nbsp;granted&nbsp;86,000&nbsp;MPSUs to the executive officers and&nbsp;41,000&nbsp;MPSUs to certain non-executive employees, which represent a target number of shares to be earned upon achievement of both market conditions and performance conditions (&ldquo;2015&nbsp;MPSUs&rdquo;). The maximum number of shares that an employee can earn is&nbsp;500%&nbsp;of the target number of the&nbsp;2015&nbsp;MPSUs. The&nbsp;2015&nbsp;MPSUs consist of&nbsp;four separate tranches with various performance periods all ending on&nbsp;December 31, 2019.&nbsp;The&nbsp;first&nbsp;tranche contains market conditions only, which require the achievement of&nbsp;five stock price targets ranging from&nbsp;$71.36&nbsp;to&nbsp;$95.57&nbsp;with a performance period from&nbsp;January 1, 2016&nbsp;to&nbsp;December 31, 2019.&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">The second,&nbsp;third&nbsp;and&nbsp;fourth&nbsp;tranches contain both market and performance conditions. The five stock price targets for the&nbsp;second&nbsp;tranche range from&nbsp;$89.56&nbsp;to&nbsp;$106.81&nbsp;with a performance period from&nbsp;January 1, 2017&nbsp;to&nbsp;December 31, 2019.&nbsp; The five stock price targets for the&nbsp;third&nbsp;tranche range from&nbsp;$120.80&nbsp;to&nbsp;$135.48&nbsp;with a performance period from&nbsp;January 1, 2018&nbsp;to&nbsp;December 31, 2019.&nbsp; The five stock price targets for the&nbsp;fourth&nbsp;tranche range from $126.08 to $136.79 with a performance period from&nbsp;January 1, 2019&nbsp;to&nbsp;December 31, 2019.</p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">In addition, each of the second,&nbsp;third&nbsp;and&nbsp;fourth&nbsp;tranches requires the achievement of&nbsp;one&nbsp;of following&nbsp;six&nbsp;operating metrics:</p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt 7.5pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<table border="0" cellpadding="0" cellspacing="0" style="width:100%;text-indent:0;font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;">

		<tr>
			<td style="vertical-align:middle;width:3.3%;">

			<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin-bottom:0pt;margin-left:0pt;margin-right:7.5pt;margin-top:0pt;text-align:left;">Successful implementation of full digital solutions for certain power products.</p>
			</td>
		</tr>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">A total of 394,000 shares have been earned by the employees as of December 31, 2018. The&nbsp;2015&nbsp;MPSUs will vest on&nbsp;January 1, 2020, with post-vesting sales restrictions on the vested shares for up to&nbsp;an additional two&nbsp;years.</p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">The Company determined the grant date fair value of the&nbsp;2015&nbsp;MPSUs using a Monte Carlo simulation model with the following weighted-average assumptions: stock price of&nbsp;$61.35,&nbsp;expected volatility of&nbsp;33.2%,&nbsp;risk-free interest rate of&nbsp;1.3%,&nbsp;and an illiquidity discount of&nbsp;7.8%&nbsp;to account for the post-vesting sales restrictions.&nbsp;Assuming the achievement of all of the required market and performance goals, the total stock-based compensation cost for the&nbsp;2015 MPSUs, excluding cancelled shares for the terminated employees, is&nbsp;$24.6&nbsp;million ($8.3&nbsp;million for the&nbsp;first&nbsp;tranche,&nbsp;$4.5&nbsp;million for the&nbsp;second&nbsp;tranche,&nbsp;$5.2&nbsp;million for the&nbsp;third&nbsp;tranche, and&nbsp;$6.6&nbsp;million for the&nbsp;fourth&nbsp;tranche).</p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">For the&nbsp;first&nbsp;tranche which contains market conditions only, stock-based compensation expense is&nbsp;being recognized over the requisite service period. For the second,&nbsp;third&nbsp;and&nbsp;fourth&nbsp;tranches, stock-based compensation expense for each tranche is recognized if an operating metric&nbsp;has been achieved, or if&nbsp;management believes it is&nbsp;probable that an operating metric will be&nbsp;achieved during the performance periods. As of December 31, 2018, two operating metrics have been achieved, and based on management&rsquo;s quarterly assessment, one additional operating metric&nbsp;was&nbsp;considered probable of being achieved during the performance period. Accordingly, stock-based compensation expense is being recognized for the second,&nbsp;third&nbsp;and&nbsp;fourth&nbsp;tranches over the requisite service period.</p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt 7.5pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;"><i><b>MSUs</b></i></p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin-bottom:0pt;margin-left:7.5pt;margin-right:0pt;margin-top:0pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;"><u>2018 MSUs:</u></p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">In&nbsp;October 2018,&nbsp;the Compensation Committee&nbsp;granted&nbsp;60,000&nbsp;MSUs to the executive officers and&nbsp;60,000&nbsp;MSUs to certain non-executive employees, which represent a target number of shares to be earned upon achievement of&nbsp;stock price targets (&ldquo;2018&nbsp;MSUs&rdquo;). The maximum number of shares that an employee can earn is&nbsp;500%&nbsp;of the target number of the&nbsp;2018&nbsp;MSUs if the Company achieves five stock price targets ranging from $140 to $172. The performance period for the first two stock price targets is from October 26, 2018 to December 31, 2021, and the performance period for the last three price targets is from October 26, 2018 to December 31, 2023. As of December 31, 2018, none of the stock price targets has been met. If the stock price targets are achieved during the performance periods, the&nbsp;2018&nbsp;MSUs will vest on&nbsp;January 1, 2024,&nbsp;with post-vesting sales restrictions on the vested shares for up to&nbsp;an additional two&nbsp;years. The total stock-based compensation cost for the 2018 MSUs is $41.1 million.</p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">The Company determined the grant date fair value of the 2018 MSUs using a Monte Carlo simulation model with the following assumptions: stock price of $108.43, expected volatility of 31.6%, a risk-free interest rate of 3.0%, and an illiquidity discount of 8.7% to account for the post-vesting sales restrictions.&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt 7.5pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin-bottom:0pt;margin-left:0pt;margin-right:7.5pt;margin-top:0pt;text-align:left;"><u>2013 MSUs:</u></p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin-bottom:0pt;margin-left:0pt;margin-right:7.5pt;margin-top:0pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin-bottom:0pt;margin-left:0pt;margin-right:7.5pt;margin-top:0pt;text-align:left;">In December 2013, the Compensation Committee&nbsp;granted 276,000 MSUs to the executive officers and 84,000 MSUs to certain non-executive employees, which represented a target number of shares that would be earned upon achievement of stock price targets (&ldquo;2013 MSUs&rdquo;). The maximum number of shares that an employee could earn was 500% of the target number of the 2013 MSUs if the Company achieved five price targets ranging from $40 to $56 during a performance period from January 1, 2014 to December 31, 2018. As of December 31, 2015, all stock price targets have been met and the employees earned a total of 1.8 million shares. The 2013 MSUs will vest quarterly from January 1, 2019 to December 31, 2023. The total stock-based compensation cost for the 2013 MSUs, excluding cancelled shares for the terminated employees, is $38.2 million.</p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin-bottom:0pt;margin-left:0pt;margin-right:7.5pt;margin-top:0pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin-bottom:0pt;margin-left:0pt;margin-right:7.5pt;margin-top:0pt;text-align:left;">The Company determined the grant date fair value of the 2013 MSUs using a Monte Carlo simulation model with the following assumptions: stock price of $31.73, expected volatility of 38.7% and a risk-free interest rate of 1.6%.&nbsp; There was no illiquidity discount because the awards do not contain any post-vesting sales restrictions.</p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt 7.5pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;&nbsp;</p>

<div id="PGNUM" style="text-align: center; width: 100%; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;">65</div>

			<td id=".trail.5" nowrap="true" style="width: 1%; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin-left: 0pt;">&nbsp;</td>
		</tr>

</table>
</div>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">Total intrinsic value of options exercised was $0.5 million, $0.7 million and $3.7 million for the years ended December 31, 2018, 2017 and 2016, respectively. Cash proceeds from stock option exercises were $0.1 million, $0.1 million and $1.3 million for the years ended December 31, 2018, 2017 and 2016, respectively.</p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt 7.5pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;"><i><b>Employee Stock Purchase Plan (&ldquo;ESPP&rdquo;)</b></i></p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">Under the ESPP, eligible employees may purchase common stock through payroll deductions. Participants may not purchase more than 2,000 shares in a six-month offering period, or purchase shares having a value greater than $25,000 in any calendar year as measured at the beginning of the offering period in accordance with the Internal Revenue Code and applicable Treasury Regulations.&nbsp;&nbsp;The ESPP provides for an annual increase by an amount equal to the least of 1.0 million shares, 2% of the outstanding shares of common stock on the first day of the year, or a number of shares as determined by the Board of Directors.&nbsp;&nbsp;As of December 31, 2018, 4.6 million shares were available for future issuance. The ESPP will expire in November 2024.</p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">For the years ended December 31, 2018, 2017 and 2016, 33,000, 40,000 and 53,000 shares, respectively, were issued under the ESPP. The intrinsic value of the shares issued&nbsp;was $1.1 million, $1.0 million and $1.0 million for the years ended December 31, 2018, 2017 and 2016, respectively. The unamortized expense as of December 31, 2018 was $0.1 million, which will be recognized through the first quarter of 2019. The Black-Scholes model was used to value the employee stock purchase rights with the following weighted-average assumptions:&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">Cash proceeds from the shares issued under the ESPP were $3.0 million, $2.7 million and $2.5 million for the years ended December 31, 2018, 2017 and 2016, respectively.</p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt 7.5pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>
</div>

<div contenteditable="false" docsect="9" id="48270" sectdesc="Note to Financial Statements">&nbsp;</div>

<div data-td-parse-id="1" data-td-statement-type="note" data-td-v2v-id="1551187625606">
<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;"><b>8. STOCK REPURCHASE PROGRAM</b></p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">In February 2016, the Board of Directors approved a stock repurchase program (the &ldquo;2016 Program&rdquo;) that authorized the Company to repurchase up to $50 million in the aggregate of its common stock through December 31, 2016. In December 2016, the Board of Directors approved an extension of the 2016 Program through December 31, 2017. The 2016 Program expired on December 31, 2017 with a remaining unused balance of $50 million. No shares were repurchased under the 2016 Program.</p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt 7.5pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>
</div>

<div contenteditable="false" docsect="9" id="48271" sectdesc="Note to Financial Statements">&nbsp;</div>

<div data-td-parse-id="1" data-td-statement-type="note" data-td-v2v-id="1551187633183">
<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;"><b>9. DIVIDENDS AND DIVIDEND EQUIVALENTS</b></p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;"><i><b>Cash Dividend Program</b></i></p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">In&nbsp;June 2014,&nbsp;the Board of Directors approved a dividend program&nbsp;pursuant to which the Company intends to pay quarterly cash dividends on its common stock. Based on the Company&rsquo;s historical practice, stockholders of record as of the last business day of the quarter are entitled to receive the quarterly cash dividends when and if declared by the Board of Directors, which are payable to the stockholders in the following month.&nbsp;The Board of Directors declared the following cash dividends (in thousands, except per-share amounts):&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<div id="PGBK" style="width: 100%; margin-left: 0pt; margin-right: 0pt">
<div id="PGNUM" style="text-align: center; width: 100%; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;">66</div>

<hr style="PAGE-BREAK-AFTER: always; border: none; width: 100%; height: 2px; color: #000000; background-color: #000000">
<div id="PGHDR" style="text-align: left; width: 100%"><a href="#toc">Table of Contents</a></div>
</div>

<p style="font-family: 'Times New Roman', Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: left">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">As of December 31, 2018 and 2017, accrued dividends totaled $12.8 million and $8.3 million, respectively.</p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">The declaration of any future cash dividends is at the discretion of the Board of Directors and will depend on, among other things, the Company&rsquo;s financial condition, results of operations, capital requirements, business conditions, and other factors that the Board of Directors&nbsp;may&nbsp;deem relevant, as well as a determination that cash dividends are in the best interests of the stockholders.</p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;"><i><b>&nbsp;</b></i></p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin-bottom:0pt;margin-left:0pt;margin-right:7.5pt;margin-top:0pt;text-align:left;">The Company anticipates&nbsp;that cash used for future dividend payments will come from its current domestic cash, cash generated from ongoing U.S. operations, and&nbsp;cash to be repatriated from its Bermuda subsidiary. Earnings from other foreign subsidiaries will continue to be indefinitely reinvested. See Note 12 for further discussion.&nbsp;<i><b>&nbsp;</b></i></p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin-bottom:0pt;margin-left:0pt;margin-right:7.5pt;margin-top:0pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin-bottom:0pt;margin-left:0pt;margin-right:7.5pt;margin-top:0pt;text-align:left;"><i><b>Cash Dividend Equivalent Rights</b></i></p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin-bottom:0pt;margin-left:16.5pt;margin-right:7.5pt;margin-top:0pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin-bottom:0pt;margin-left:0pt;margin-right:7.5pt;margin-top:0pt;text-align:left;">Under the Company&rsquo;s stock plans, outstanding RSUs contain rights to receive cash dividend equivalents, which entitle employees who hold RSUs to the same dividend value per share as holders of common stock. The dividend equivalents are accumulated and paid to the employees when the underlying RSUs vest. Dividend equivalents accumulated on the underlying RSUs are forfeited if the employees do&nbsp;not&nbsp;fulfill their service requirement and the awards do&nbsp;not&nbsp;vest.&nbsp;As of&nbsp;December 31, 2018 and 2017,&nbsp;accrued dividend equivalents totaled&nbsp;$8.4&nbsp;million and&nbsp;$5.8&nbsp;million, respectively.&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt 7.5pt;text-align:center;">&nbsp;</p>
</div>

<div contenteditable="false" docsect="9" id="48272" sectdesc="Note to Financial Statements">&nbsp;</div>

<div contenteditable="false" docsect="9" id="48273" sectdesc="Note to Financial Statements">&nbsp;</div>

<div data-td-parse-id="1" data-td-statement-type="note" data-td-v2v-id="1551187647790">
<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin-bottom:0pt;margin-left:0pt;margin-right:7.5pt;margin-top:0pt;text-align:left;"><b>11.&nbsp;&nbsp; NET INCOME PER SHARE</b></p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin-bottom:0pt;margin-left:0pt;margin-right:7.5pt;margin-top:0pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

		</tr>
		<tr style="vertical-align: bottom; background-color: rgb(204, 238, 255);">
			<td style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;">
			<p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin-top: 0pt; margin-bottom: 0pt; margin-left: 18pt;">Undistributed foreign earnings</p>

			<td id=".amt.2" style="width: 14%; text-align: right; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin-left: 0pt; border-bottom: 3px double rgb(0, 0, 0);">16,830</td>
			<td id=".trail.2" nowrap="true" style="width: 1%; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; padding-bottom: 3px; margin-left: 0pt;">&nbsp;</td>

			<td id=".amt.3" style="width: 14%; text-align: right; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin-left: 0pt; border-bottom: 3px double rgb(0, 0, 0);">15,917</td>
			<td id=".trail.3" nowrap="true" style="width: 1%; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; padding-bottom: 3px; margin-left: 0pt;">&nbsp;</td>

</div>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;<i><b>&nbsp;</b></i></p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;"><i><b>2017</b></i><i><b> </b></i><i><b>Tax Act</b></i></p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">Under ASC No. 740, the effects of a new legislation are recognized upon enactment. Accordingly, the Company was required to recognize the tax effects of the 2017 Tax Act beginning in the fourth quarter of 2017.</p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">On December 22, 2017, the SEC issued SAB 118, which addressed the application of ASC No. 740 in situations when a registrant did not have the necessary information available, prepared, or analyzed in reasonable detail to complete the accounting for certain income tax effects of the 2017 Tax Act. In accordance with SAB 118, for matters that have not been completed, the Company would recognize provisional amounts to the extent that they were reasonably estimable. Any subsequent adjustments to the provisional amounts would be recorded to the income tax provision in the period when the analysis was complete. The Company was permitted to finalize the analysis within a one-year measurement period.</p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">As of December 31, 2017, the Company had not completed the accounting for the tax effects of the 2017 Tax Act and recorded certain provisional amounts based on reasonable estimates. In December 2018, the Company finalized the analysis of the 2017 Tax Act and recorded certain adjustments to the provisional amounts, as discussed further below.</p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">The Company expects further guidance may be forthcoming from the FASB and the SEC, as well as regulations, interpretations and rulings from federal and state tax agencies, which could result in additional impact and adjustments to the Company&rsquo;s income tax provisions in future periods.&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt 7.5pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;"><u>Corporate Tax Rate and Remeasurement of Deferred Taxes:</u></p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">The 2017 Tax Act reduces the corporate tax rate from 35% to 21%, effective January 1, 2018. Because ASC No. 740 requires the effect of a change in tax laws to be recognized as of the date of enactment, the Company remeasured its deferred tax balance as of December 22, 2017 and recorded a provisional amount of $9.8 million to the income tax provision as a result of the remeasurement for the year ended December 31, 2017. In December 2018, the Company finalized the analysis and did not make any adjustment to the provisional amount recorded for the year ended December 31, 2017.</p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt 7.5pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;"><u>Deemed Repatriation Transition Tax:</u></p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">The 2017 Tax Act mandates a one-time deemed repatriation transition tax of post-1986 undistributed foreign earnings and profits (&ldquo;E&amp;P&rdquo;) on which U.S. income taxes were previously deferred. For the year ended&nbsp;December 31, 2017,&nbsp;the Company recorded a provisional amount of&nbsp;$41.9&nbsp;million related to the transition tax expense. After the utilization of R&amp;D tax credits of&nbsp;$18.0&nbsp;million, the provisional transition tax liability was&nbsp;$23.9 million.&nbsp;&nbsp;In December 2018, the Company finalized the analysis of the transition tax, and recorded an increase of $1.3 million to the transition tax expense and a net&nbsp;increase of $0.7 million to the transition tax liability.</p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">As permitted by the 2017 Tax Act, the Company has elected to pay the transition tax liability of $24.6 million in installments on an&nbsp;interest-free basis&nbsp;over eight years through 2025. For the year ended December 31, 2018, the Company paid $2.6 million of the transition tax. As of December 31, 2018, $1.3 million of the remaining transition tax was recorded in current accrued liabilities and $20.7 million was recorded in long-term income tax liabilities.</p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt 7.5pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<div id="PGBK" style="width: 100%; margin-left: 0pt; margin-right: 0pt">
<div id="PGNUM" style="text-align: center; width: 100%; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;">69</div>

<hr style="PAGE-BREAK-AFTER: always; border: none; width: 100%; height: 2px; color: #000000; background-color: #000000">
<div id="PGHDR" style="text-align: left; width: 100%"><a href="#toc">Table of Contents</a></div>
</div>

<p style="font-family: 'Times New Roman', Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: left">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;"><u>Undistributed Earnings of Subsidiaries:</u></p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">The Company previously considered the earnings in its non-U.S. subsidiaries to be indefinitely reinvested and, accordingly, recorded no deferred income taxes. Prior to the transition tax, the Company had an excess of the amount for financial reporting over the tax basis in its foreign subsidiaries including undistributed foreign earnings of $390.2 million. While the transition tax resulted in the reduction of the excess of the amount for financial reporting over the tax basis in its foreign subsidiaries and subjected $123.2&nbsp;million of undistributed foreign earnings to tax, an actual repatriation from its non-U.S. subsidiaries could be subject to additional foreign withholding taxes and U.S. state taxes.</p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">The Company has analyzed its global working capital and cash requirements, and has determined that it plans to repatriate cash from its Bermuda subsidiary on an ongoing basis to fund its future U.S. based expenditures and dividends. For the other foreign subsidiaries, the Company expects to indefinitely reinvest undistributed earnings to fund foreign operations and their research and development. As of December 31, 2018, the Company recorded deferred taxes liabilities of $1.1 million related to California state taxes, which were netted against deferred tax assets.</p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt 7.5pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;"><u>GILTI</u><u>:</u></p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;"><i><b>&nbsp;</b></i></p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">The 2017 Tax Act subjects a U.S. parent shareholder to taxation of its GILTI, effective January 1, 2018. The GILTI inclusions impact companies that have foreign earnings generated without a large aggregate foreign fixed asset base and whose earnings are being taxed at a low tax rate. For the year ended December 31, 2018, the Company included $81.1 million related to the GILTI&nbsp;provisions&nbsp;as additional Subpart F income, which was&nbsp;accounted for&nbsp;as a period cost.</p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;"><u>Executive Compensation Deductions:</u></p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">The 2017 Tax Act retains the $1 million limitation on deductible compensation to covered employees, which include the Chief Executive Officer and four other highest paid officers, under IRC Section 162(m). However, it eliminates the exception for performance-based cash or stock compensation and expands the definition of covered employees to include the Chief Financial Officer. Accordingly, beginning January 1, 2018, the deductible compensation to covered employees is generally subject to the $1 million limitation.</p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt 7.5pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;"><i><b>Release of Valuation Allowance</b></i></p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;"><i><b>&nbsp;</b></i></p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">Management periodically evaluates the realizability of the Company&rsquo;s deferred tax assets based on all available evidence. The realizability of the Company&rsquo;s net deferred tax assets is dependent on its ability to generate sufficient future taxable income during periods prior to the expiration of tax attributes to fully utilize these assets. The Company maintained a full valuation allowance on its U.S. deferred tax assets as of the third quarter of 2017.&nbsp;&nbsp;In the fourth quarter of 2017, the Company assessed the realizability of the deferred tax assets and concluded that it was more likely than not that its federal deferred tax assets would be realizable, due principally to the enactment of the 2017 Tax Act.</p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">In accordance with ASC No. 740, management considered all available evidence, both positive and negative, to determine whether, based on the weight of that evidence, a valuation allowance for deferred tax assets was needed. The Company&rsquo;s conclusion was primarily driven by the following positive evidence:</p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<table border="0" cellpadding="0" cellspacing="0" style="width:100%;text-indent:0;font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;">

		<tr>
			<td style="vertical-align:middle;width:3.3%;">
			<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>
			</td>
			<td style="vertical-align:top;width:3.3%;">
			<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">&#9679;</p>
			</td>
			<td style="vertical-align:top;width:93.3%;">
			<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">The Company forecasted&nbsp;taxable income in the U.S.&nbsp;in future periods.&nbsp; The&nbsp;enactment of GILTI will result in additional Subpart F income each year.</p>
			</td>
		</tr>

</table>

<table border="0" cellpadding="0" cellspacing="0" style="width:100%;text-indent:0;font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;">

		<tr>
			<td style="vertical-align:middle;width:3.3%;">
			<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>
			</td>
			<td style="vertical-align:top;width:3.3%;">
			<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">&#9679;</p>
			</td>
			<td style="vertical-align:top;width:93.3%;">
			<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">Executive performance-based equity awards are now subject to the Section 162(m) deduction limitation.</p>
			</td>
		</tr>

</table>

<table border="0" cellpadding="0" cellspacing="0" style="width:100%;text-indent:0;font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;">

		<tr>
			<td style="vertical-align:middle;width:3.3%;">
			<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>
			</td>
			<td style="vertical-align:top;width:3.3%;">
			<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">&#9679;</p>
			</td>
			<td style="vertical-align:top;width:93.3%;">
			<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">The Company has a history of utilizing all federal tax attributes before expiration.</p>
			</td>
		</tr>

</table>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">As a result, the Company released $21.6 million of valuation allowance on federal deferred tax assets, which was recorded as a benefit in the income tax provision in the fourth quarter of 2017. The Company continues to maintain a full valuation allowance on the deferred tax assets in California, primarily due to a low apportionment factor and the amount of R&amp;D tax credits generated is greater than the amount utilized.</p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin-bottom:0pt;margin-left:7.5pt;margin-right:8.4pt;margin-top:0pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;"><i><b>Other Income Tax Provision Matters</b></i></p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">As of December 31, 2018, the Company did not have federal net operating loss carryforwards. As of December 31, 2018, the state net operating loss carryforwards for income tax purposes were $16.2 million, which will expire beginning in 2027.</p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">As of December 31, 2018, the Company had no R&amp;D tax credit carryforwards for federal income tax purposes, and $24.1 million for state income tax purposes, which can be carried forward indefinitely.</p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;</p>

<div id="PGBK" style="width: 100%; margin-left: 0pt; margin-right: 0pt">
<div id="PGNUM" style="text-align: center; width: 100%; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;">70</div>

<hr style="PAGE-BREAK-AFTER: always; border: none; width: 100%; height: 2px; color: #000000; background-color: #000000">
<div id="PGHDR" style="text-align: left; width: 100%"><a href="#toc">Table of Contents</a></div>
</div>

<p style="font-family: 'Times New Roman', Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: left">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">In the event of a change in ownership, as defined under federal and state tax laws, the Company&#39;s net operating loss and tax credit carryforwards could be subject to annual limitations.&nbsp;&nbsp;The annual limitations could result in the expiration of the net operating loss and tax credit carryforwards prior to utilization.</p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">In July 2018, the U.S. Ninth Circuit Court of Appeals overturned the U.S. Tax Court&rsquo;s unanimous 2015 decision in&nbsp;<i>Altera v. Commissioner</i>, holding that the Internal Revenue Service&nbsp;(&quot;IRS&quot;) did not violate the rule-making procedures required by the Administrative Procedures Act. In the case<i>,&nbsp;</i>the taxpayer challenged IRS&nbsp;regulations that required participants in qualified cost sharing arrangements&nbsp;to share stock based compensation costs. The Tax Court had invalidated those regulations, in part because the Treasury Department failed to adequately consider significant taxpayer comments when adopting them. In August 2018, the U.S. Ninth Circuit Court of Appeals withdrew its July 2018 opinion. At this time, the Treasury Department has not withdrawn the requirement from its regulations to include stock-based compensation in the cost pool to be shared under a cost-sharing arrangement. Due to the uncertainty surrounding the status of the current regulations, questions related to the scope of potential benefits, and the risk of the Tax Court&rsquo;s decision being overturned upon appeal, the Company has not recorded any adjustments as of December 31, 2018. The Company will continue to monitor developments related to this case&nbsp;and the potential impact on its financial statements.</p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">At December 31, 2018, the Company had $20.5 million of unrecognized tax benefits, $12.8 million of which would affect its effective tax rate if recognized after considering the valuation allowance. At December 31, 2017, the Company had $16.3 million of unrecognized tax benefits, $9.1 million of which would affect its effective tax rate if recognized after considering the valuation allowance.&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt 7.5pt;text-align:center;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin-bottom:0pt;margin-left:0pt;margin-right:7.5pt;margin-top:0pt;text-align:left;">A reconciliation of the gross unrecognized tax benefits is as follows (in thousands):&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">The Company recognizes interest and penalties, if any, related to uncertain tax positions in its income tax provision. As of December 31, 2018 and 2017, the Company has $0.9 million and $0.5 million, respectively, of accrued interest related to uncertain tax positions, which were recorded in long-term income tax liabilities in the Consolidated Balance Sheets.</p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">Uncertain tax positions relate to the allocation of income and deductions among the Company&rsquo;s global entities and to the determination of the research and development tax credit. It is reasonably possible that over the next twelve-month period, the Company may experience increases or decreases in its unrecognized tax benefits. However, it is not possible to determine either the magnitude or the range of increases or decreases at this time.</p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">The Company currently has reduced tax rates in its subsidiaries in Chengdu and Hangzhou, China for performing research and development activities through 2020 and 2019, respectively. In addition, the Company had a tax holiday in Switzerland, which expired on December 31, 2018 &nbsp;The tax holiday and tax incentives had an insignificant impact on earnings per share for the periods presented.</p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt 7.5pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;"><i><b>Income Tax Examination</b></i></p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">The Company is subject to examination of its income tax returns by the IRS and other tax authorities. The Company&rsquo;s U.S. federal income tax return for the year ended December 31, 2014 was under examination by the IRS in 2016. In January 2017, the IRS completed its examination with no adjustments.</p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt 7.5pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>
</div>

		</tr>

</table>
</div>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<div id="PGBK" style="width: 100%; margin-left: 0pt; margin-right: 0pt">
<div id="PGNUM" style="text-align: center; width: 100%; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;">71</div>

<hr style="PAGE-BREAK-AFTER: always; border: none; width: 100%; height: 2px; color: #000000; background-color: #000000">

<p style="font-family: 'Times New Roman', Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: left">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">The Company leases manufacturing and inventory warehouse facilities, sales and marketing, and research and development offices in Asia, Europe and the United States. Certain of the Company&rsquo;s facility leases provide for periodic rent increases. Rent expense for the years ended December 31, 2018, 2017 and 2016 was $1.8 million, $1.5 million and $1.7 million, respectively.</p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt 7.5pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin-bottom:0pt;margin-left:0pt;margin-right:7.5pt;margin-top:0pt;text-align:left;"><i><b>Warranty and Indemnification Provisions</b></i></p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin-bottom:0pt;margin-left:0pt;margin-right:7.5pt;margin-top:0pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin-bottom:0pt;margin-left:0pt;margin-right:7.5pt;margin-top:0pt;text-align:left;">The changes in warranty reserves are as follows (in thousands):</p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">The Company provides indemnification agreements to certain direct or indirect customers. The Company agrees to reimburse these parties for any damages, costs and expenses incurred by them as a result of legal actions taken against them by third parties for infringing upon their intellectual property rights as a result of using the Company&rsquo;s products and technologies. These indemnification provisions are varied in their scope and are subject to certain terms, conditions, limitations and exclusions. In addition, the Company has entered into indemnification agreements with its directors and officers.</p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">It is not possible to predict the maximum potential amount of future payments under these agreements due to the limited history of indemnification claims and the unique facts and circumstances involved in each particular agreement. There were no indemnification liabilities incurred in any of the periods presented. However, there can be no assurances that the Company will not incur any financial liabilities in the future as a result of these obligations.</p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt 7.5pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>
</div>

<div contenteditable="false" docsect="9" id="48276" sectdesc="Note to Financial Statements">&nbsp;</div>

<div data-td-parse-id="1" data-td-statement-type="note" data-td-v2v-id="1551187671443">
<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;"><b>14.&nbsp;&nbsp;LITIGATION</b></p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">The Company is a party to actions and proceedings in the ordinary course of business, including potential litigation initiated by its shareholders, challenges to the enforceability or validity of its intellectual property, claims that the Company&rsquo;s products infringe on the intellectual property rights of others, and employment matters. These proceedings often involve complex questions of fact and law and<i>&nbsp;</i>may&nbsp;require the expenditure of significant funds and the diversion of other resources to prosecute and defend. The Company defends itself vigorously against any such claims. As of&nbsp;December 31, 2018,&nbsp;there were&nbsp;no&nbsp;material pending legal proceedings to which the Company was a party.</p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>
</div>

<div contenteditable="false" docsect="9" id="48277" sectdesc="Note to Financial Statements">&nbsp;</div>

<div data-td-parse-id="1" data-td-statement-type="note" data-td-v2v-id="1551187678962">
<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;"><b>15.&nbsp;&nbsp;EMPLOYEE 401(k) PLAN</b></p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">The Company sponsors a 401(k) retirement savings plan for all employees in the U.S. who meet certain eligibility requirements. Participants may contribute up to the amount allowable as a deduction for federal income tax purposes. The Company is not required to contribute and did not contribute to the plan for the years ended December 31, 2018, 2017 and 2016.</p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt 7.5pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>
</div>

<div contenteditable="false" docsect="9" id="48278" sectdesc="Note to Financial Statements">&nbsp;</div>

<div data-td-parse-id="1" data-td-statement-type="note" data-td-v2v-id="1551187686487">
<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin-bottom:0pt;margin-left:0pt;margin-right:7.5pt;margin-top:0pt;text-align:left;"><b>16.&nbsp;&nbsp;SIGNIFICANT CUSTOMERS</b></p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin-bottom:0pt;margin-left:0pt;margin-right:7.5pt;margin-top:0pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin-bottom:0pt;margin-left:0pt;margin-right:7.5pt;margin-top:0pt;text-align:left;">The Company sells its products primarily through&nbsp;third-party distributors and value-added resellers, and directly to original equipment manufacturers, original design manufacturers and electronic manufacturing service providers. The following table summarizes&nbsp;those customers with sales equal to or greater than&nbsp;10%&nbsp;of the Company&#39;s total revenue, or with accounts receivable balances greater than&nbsp;10%&nbsp;of the Company&rsquo;s total accounts receivable:</p>

<p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin-bottom:0pt;margin-left:0pt;margin-right:7.5pt;margin-top:0pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<div data-td-parse-id="1" data-td-taggable-table="table" data-td-v2v-id="1551187686486">
<table border="0" cellpadding="0" cellspacing="0" class="finTable" style="width: 100%; font-size: 10pt; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; text-indent: 0px;">

		<tr style="vertical-align: bottom;">
			<td style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;">&nbsp;</td>
			<td id=".lead.D4" style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;">&nbsp;</td>
			<td colspan="10" id=".amt.D4" style="text-align: center; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin-left: 0pt; border-bottom: 1px solid rgb(0, 0, 0);">

<div id="PGNUM" style="text-align: center; width: 100%; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;">72</div>

<hr style="PAGE-BREAK-AFTER: always; border: none; width: 100%; height: 2px; color: #000000; background-color: #000000">
<div id="PGHDR" style="text-align: left; width: 100%"><a href="#toc">Table of Contents</a></div>
